person.name: Margaret Gray
person.alternateName: Margaret de la Haye (Gray) of Broxmouth
person.userCertifiedLifeSummary:
«b»Biography«/b»
Margaret Gray

Father Sir Patrick Gray d. a 1373
Mother Margaret

Margaret Gray married Sir William de la Haye, 1st Lord Hay, 8th Baron Erroll, Constable of Scotland, son of Sir Thomas de la Haye, 7th Baron Erroll, Constable of Scotland and Elizabeth Stewart;

They had 5 sons (Gilbert; William of Ury; Walter; David, Master of Hay; & Thomas, Master of Hay) Margaret Gray was born at of Broxmouth, Roxburghshire, Scotland.

«b»Children of Sir William de la Haye, 1st Lord Hay and Margaret Gray:«/b»
1.) Gilbert, who succeeded; d. 7 Sep 1436

2.) William of Ury in Kinkardineshire, charter from his father in the barony of Cowie 20 July 1430; d. shortly before 02 Feb 1471/2.

3.) Walter, a witness with brothers David & Thomas 14 Mar 1470/1.

4.) David

Thomas

zh.Yester, East Lothian, Scotland: 蘇格蘭哈丁頓史爾也斯特
zh.lifeSummary: 當Margaret Gray於1363年出生在蘇格蘭洛包爾時，她的父親Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth33歲，母親Margaret Boxmouth,29歲。她於1390年之前與Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay結婚。他們至少是1個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1432年在蘇格蘭培爾培斯享年69歲，埋葬在蘇格蘭培爾培斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Margaret Gray于1363年出生在苏格兰洛包爾时，她的父亲Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth33岁，母亲Margaret Boxmouth,29岁。她于1390年之前与Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay结婚。他们至少是1个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1432年在苏格兰培爾培斯享年69岁，埋葬在苏格兰培爾培斯。

nameMeanings.name: Gray
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish (especially Eastern Ulster; of Norman origin): habitational name from Graye in Calvados, France, named from the Gallo-Roman personal name Graec(i)us, meaning ‘Greek’ + the locative suffix -acum. This is probably the chief source of the surname in Britain.

English: nickname for someone with gray hair or a gray beard, from Middle English grey (Old English grǣg, grēg) ‘gray’. In Ireland it has been used as a translation of various Gaelic surnames derived from riabhach ‘brindled, gray’, including Mac Giolla Riabhaigh; see McGreevy . In North America, this surname has assimilated names with similar meaning from other languages.
French: habitational name from Gray in Haute-Saône or Le Gray in Seine-Maritime.

variants.name: McGreevy
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Seamus, Brendan.
Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mag Riabhaigh, a patronymic from the personal name Riabhach ‘brindled, grizzled’. Compare Reavey and Creevy 2.

variants.name: Grayer
meanings: English (southern): nickname from Middle English grei ‘gray’ (see Gray ) + her ‘hair’.

variants.name: Grays
meanings: English (Durham): variant of Gray , with post-medieval excrescent -s, or of Grace .

variants.name: Grice
meanings: English (Midlands): from Middle English grice, grise ‘pig’ (Old Norse gríss), probably a nickname or a metonymic occupational name for a swineherd.
English: nickname for a gray-haired man, from Middle English grice, gris ‘gray’ (Old French gris, apparently of ancient Germanic origin, and probably a distant cognate of Gray 1).
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Greis  .

variants.name: Grey
meanings: English, Scottish, and Irish: variant of   Gray  .
In some cases also an American shortened and altered form of Ukrainian Grushko: nickname or topographic name derived from a Russian-influenced transliteration of grushka ‘pear, pear tree’.

nameMeanings.name: Margaret
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

variants.name: Margareta
meanings: From the Latin form of Greek Margarītēs. See Margaret .

variants.name: Margaretta
meanings: An extremely common given name from the Middle Ages onwards, derived via Old French Marguerite and Latin Margarita from Greek Margarītēs, from margaron ‘pearl’, a word ultimately of Hebrew origin. The name was always understood to mean ‘pearl’ throughout the Middle Ages. The first St Margaret was martyred at Antioch in Pisidia during the persecution instigated by the Emperor Diocletian in the early 4th century. However, there is some doubt about her name, as the same saint is venerated in the Orthodox Church as Marina . There were several other saintly bearers of the name, including St Margaret of Scotland ( d. 1093 ), wife of King Malcolm Canmore and daughter of Edmund Ironside of England. It was also the name of the wife of Henry VI of England, Margaret of Anjou ( 1430–82 ), and of Margaret Tudor ( 1489–1541 ), sister of Henry VIII , who married James IV of Scotland and ruled as regent there after his death. See also Margery , Marjorie .

en.lifeSummary: When Margaret Gray was born in 1363, in Roxburghshire, Scotland, her father, Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth, was 33 and her mother, Margaret Boxmouth,, was 29. She married Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay before 1390. They were the parents of at least 1 son and 1 daughter. She died in 1432, in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, at the age of 69, and was buried in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Margaret Gray est née en 1363, à Roxburghshire, Écosse, son père, Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth, avait 33 ans et sa mère, Margaret Boxmouth,, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay avant 1390. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1432, à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse, à l’âge de 69 ans, et a été enterrée à Perth, Perthshire, Écosse.

de.lifeSummary: Als Margaret Gray 1363 in Roxburghshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth 33 und ihre Mutter Margaret Boxmouth, 29. Sie hat vor 1390 Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Sohn und 1 Tochter. Sie starb 1432 im Alter von 69 Jahren in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland und wurde in Perth, Perthshire, Scotland begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Margaret Gray è nata il nel 1363 nel Roxburghshire, Scotland, sua padre, Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth, aveva 33 anni e sua madre, Margaret Boxmouth,, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay prima del 1390. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlio e 1 figlia. Morì nel 1432, nel Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, all'età di 69 anni, e fu sepolta a Perth, Perthshire, Scotland.

ja.lifeSummary: Margaret Grayが1363年にスコットランドロクスブルグhシュアイアで生まれた時，父Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouthは33歳，母Margaret Boxmouth,は29歳であった。1390年以前にSir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hayと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は1432年にスコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスで69歳で死去し，スコットランドペルスシュアイアペルスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Margaret Gray(이)가 1363년 스컷랜드 록스버그h실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth(은)는 33세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Margaret Boxmouth,(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1390년 이전 Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1432년 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에서 69세의 나이로 사망했으며 스컷랜드 퍼스실르 퍼스에 묻혔다.

Ela casou-se com Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay antes 1390. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filho e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em 1432, em Perth, Perthshire, Scotland, com 69 anos, e foi sepultada em Perth, Perthshire, Scotland.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Margaret Gray родилась в 1363 г. в местности роксбургшир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir Patrick Gray Laird of Broxmouth было 33 года, а матери, которую звали Margaret Boxmouth,, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Sir William de la Haye 1st Lord Hay перед 1390 г. У них был по крайней мере 1 сын и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1432 г. в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия в возрасте 69 лет и была похоронена в местности пирт, пиртшир, Шотландия.
